steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage claims that endothermy allowed mosasaurs to inhabit cold polar waters, implying a direct link between being endothermic and presence in those regions. The strongest clue is "include relatively cold polar waters in their range". Evidence must directly connect their presence in these areas to their physiology.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice C provides direct fossil evidence placing mosasaurs in polar regions while showing nonendothermic reptiles were less common there—exactly what the physiology claim predicts. A discusses data methodology (unrelated to habitat), B contradicts the prediction by citing equal presence, and D weakens the claim by suggesting polar waters weren't as cold. That matches choice C — C is correct.
